title: Thandai Smoothie
description: Cool Holi-inspired almond-spice smoothie with plant milk. Festive, refreshing, and caffeine-free.
explanation: Traditional thandai is a spiced nut drink for Holi and warm afternoons. This smoothie version uses soaked nuts, fennel, cardamom, pepper, and plant milk—fully plant-based and blender-friendly.
Serve cold over ice for festivals, or as a weekend craving drink. Adjust pepper for a gentle heat.
ingredients: 2 cups Cold Soy or Almond Milk
Rose petals or extra nuts to garnish (optional)
instructions: Blend soaked almonds, seeds, fennel, cardamom seeds, pepper, sweetener, and 1/2 cup milk to a smooth paste.
Add remaining cold milk, saffron, and ice. Blend until frothy.
Taste for sweetness and spice. Strain for a silkier drink if desired.
stepNotes: Soaking almonds 2–4 hours (or overnight) improves creaminess.
Freshly cracked cardamom and pepper taste brighter than old ground spices.
Straining is optional—unstrained is more filling and higher fiber.
Keep pepper mild for kids; adults often like a noticeable kick.
Chill glasses for a festival presentation.

variationSections.title: Festival service
items: Make a thandai paste concentrate ahead; thin with milk when guests arrive.
Offer a sugar-free version sweetened only with dates.
Add a tiny pinch of dried ginger for winter evenings.

variationSections.title: Protein boost
items: Use soy milk as the base.
Add a scoop of unflavored or vanilla plant protein.
Include hemp seeds in the blend.
